sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.13 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Suppose that$G$is a periodic group with an involution$i$such that$i^g i$has odd order for any$g G$. Is it true that the image of$i$in$G/O(G)$belongs to the centre of$G/O(G)$? { A.\,V.\,Borovik} No, not always (E.\,B.\,Durakov, A.\,I.\,Sozutov, Algebra Logic, { 52}, no.\,5 (2013), 422--425).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
